🔴 **__VCs to AI Startups: Please Take Our Money__**

The fundraising script has *flipped *for a few dozen of the top AI startups: __VCs are pitching to them and presenting them with gifts and favors in hopes of leading their next round. __

The best companies are getting preempted every round, and the time between rounds is shrinking. The surge in preemptive deals and soaring valuations has kindled fears of an AI bubble. Some founders have been wary of accepting offers as they risk pricing themselves out of future M&A opportunities or locking themselves into aggressive growth expectations if they raise cash at unsustainable levels. 

-# [Source](<https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2025-09-23/vcs-are-scrambling-for-a-piece-of-ai-darlings-like-anthropic-cursor-cognition>)

@🥸news 🟦 The company behind **Discord Support** suffered a __data breach__.

Discord's official statement:
```
[...] [leaked data] may include:

- Your name, Discord username, email and other contact details if you provided them
- Limited payment information, including payment type, last four digits of your credit card, and purchase history if associated with your account
- IP addresses
- Messages and attachments sent to our Customer Support or Trust & Safety agents

The incident did not include:
- Full credit card numbers or CCV codes
- Your physical address
- Your messages or activity on Discord beyond what you may have discussed with customer support or trust and safety agents
- Your Discord password or authentication data
```

@🥸news :tailwindcss: **Tailwind Labs** has laid off **75% of its engineering team**.

The reason is painfully ironic: Tailwind became extremely popular with **AI coding agents** (≈75M downloads/month), which meant fewer humans visited the docs, the main place where Tailwind promotes its paid products.

As a result, docs traffic dropped ~40% since early 2023 and revenue fell nearly **80%**, despite Tailwind being more widely used than ever.

-# Sources: [X](https://x.com/i/status/2009132572794835201), [GitHub comment](https://github.com/tailwindlabs/tailwindcss.com/pull/2388#issuecomment-3717222957)

> Z.ai, formerly known as Ziphu AI, the developer of the GLM family of large language models (LLMs), made its public market debut on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, becoming, as investors describe, the world’s first publicly listed large language model company.

> The company, which trades under the ticker 02513.HK, priced its shares at HK$116.20 apiece and opened at HK$120.00, giving it a market capitalisation of approximately HK$52.83 billion, or $6.8 billion. With the listing, the company raised roughly $558 million, according to a press release from Qiming Venture Partners, one of the company’s early backers.

> Founded in 2019, Z.ai develops open-weight LLMs (allowing users to customise models for specific tasks) that, across multiple benchmarks, have matched or exceeded the performance of both open-source and proprietary models from the United States, while competing closely with Chinese peers such as DeepSeek and Alibaba’s Qwen series.
https://analyticsindiamag.com/ai-news-updates/worlds-first-llm-company-goes-public/

@🥸news 🇮🇳 **India has warned Apple it will proceed with its antitrust lawsuit** after the company delayed responses to regulators.

The lawsuit centers on Apple’s control over the iOS app ecosystem. India’s Competition Commission found that Apple forces developers to use its in-app payment system, restricts alternative app distribution, and charges commissions of up to 30%, which the regulator says unfairly disadvantages developers and competing payment providers.

Apple had asked India to pause the investigation while it challenged earlier penalties in court, but regulators rejected the request and said the probe will continue. If fines are calculated on global turnover, Apple could face penalties of up to **$38 billion**.

-# Sources: [Reuters](<https://www.reuters.com/sustainability/boards-policy-regulation/india-warns-apple-it-will-proceed-with-antitrust-case-after-company-plays-time-2026-01-15/>)

@🥸news ~~Nothing has rebranded~~ **Nothing has copied Jaguar's rebranding**, __even copying the font__, despite the huge backlash Jaguar faced during there rebranding. As another company moves away from their iconic branding to a minimal, generic theme, the internet is MAD 😠

@🥸news 🦀 X has open-sourced its new recommendation algorithm, revealing a shift to a Rust-heavy architecture.

The new repository, x-algorithm, is a complete rewrite of the platform's ranking logic. The codebase is approximately 63% Rust, marking a move away from legacy infrastructure toward high-performance systems programming.

The core of the new system is "Phoenix," which relies on a Grok-based Transformer to rank content. The documentation confirms the removal of all hand-engineered features, effectively treating user interactions as a sequence modeling problem. This places the focus entirely on the serving infrastructure required to run Transformer inference on millions of candidates in real-time.

-# Sources: [GitHub Repo](https://github.com/xai-org/x-algorithm) | [Announcement Tweet](https://x.com/XEng/status/2013471689087086804?s=20)

messages.content: Rip scala 🤧🥀💔

@🥸news 🤡 Reset the counter. Node.js has fallen... again.

​It is January 2026, and we are starting the year strong with a kill switch built into the runtime itself.

# ​The Newest L:

A critical DoS vulnerability (CVE-2025-59466) in async_hooks means that a simple stack overflow now instantly kills your process with exit code 7.

- **​The Absurdity:** V8 (Chrome's engine) handles stack exhaustion by "crashing the tab." Node.js inherited this. When async_hooks tracks a recursive function (common in APMs like Datadog or OpenTelemetry), the stack gets too heavy, V8 panics, and Node.js just... leaves.
​
- **The Result:** No try/catch. No error log. Just a hard exit. Your server vanishes because a function called itself one too many times.

- **​"But I don't use async_hooks!"** — Yes you do. If you use Next.js, Datadog, New Relic, or OpenTelemetry, you are vulnerable.

- ​**The Fix:** Update Node.js immediately or your server dies the moment a recursive function gets too excited.

# The "Year in Review" (JS Hall of Shame):

At this point, using npm in production is just recreational gambling. Look at this timeline from the last few months:

- ​**Jan 2026 (Now):** The Runtime itself has a self-destruct button.

- ​**Nov 2025:** Shai-Hulud v2 – A self-replicating worm that infected 800+ packages because we still run untrusted postinstall scripts for some reason.

- **Oct 2024:** LottieFiles – The animation library used by everyone got compromised to drain crypto wallets.

- ​**Sep 2025:** The Chalk/Debug Incident – The most downloaded packages on Earth were hijacked to steal crypto keys. If you had console.log() in your app, you were mining for a hacker.

- ​**Aug 2025:** Nx Supply Chain Attack – Another build tool compromised.

- ​**Mid-2025:** Polyfill.io – The gift that kept on giving (malware to millions of sites).

# ​The Takeaway:

We are building "enterprise grade" software on a Jenga tower held together by duct tape and prayers. If you needed a sign to finally port that backend to Go or Rust or some other stack, maybe this is it.

# Sources: [Node.js Security Blog](https://nodejs.org/en/blog/vulnerability/january-2026-dos-mitigation-async-hooks) | [Node.js Security Release](https://nodejs.org/en/blog/vulnerability/december-2025-security-releases?hl=en-IN) | [Explanation by Low Level](https://youtu.be/y2ujgpIZ5O8?si=-Hg5j-eAmcChpyqL)

@🥸news  🟠 A bit old now, but the crazy saga around **Anna’s Archive** keeps bubbling.

A few days ago, **Spotify + Universal, Sony & Warner** slapped a **$13 TRILLION** lawsuit on the shadow library over alleged **scraping of 86M music files + tons of metadata** — calling it “brazen theft” of most of the world’s commercial songs.

The courts actually **issued injunctions and took down some of Anna’s Archive’s domains** after the labels went after hosting providers and registries to stop distribution.

Meanwhile, there’s also been **legal drama around the Internet Archive** (different org) from years back — big publishers sued it over ebook lending, that case ended with injunctions and some books pulled from loan because of copyright rulings.

So… is this the end of shadow libraries? Dunno. Anna’s Archive still pops up on mirrors, the music hasn’t widely leaked, and the whole thing touches on massive copyright vs preservation debates. Maybe it’ll get buried in legal limbo or become the next major AI training dataset controversy 👀

-# Sources: [NME Spotify lawsuit](https://www.nme.com/news/music/spotify-major-record-labels-sue-annas-archive-13trillion-allege-brazen-theft-millions-files-3925977)

embeds.title: Spotify and the three main major record labels sue Anna's Archive f...
embeds.description: Spotify and three major record labels have sued Anna's Archive for $13trillion, alleging that the platform scraped 86million music files.

messages.content:
@🥸news A slightly wild one: SpaceX has folded **xAI** into its corporate structure, effectively merging Musk’s rocket + satellite empire with his AI company (the one behind Grok). Reports say the combined entity is being valued at around **$1.25 trillion**, which is absurd for a mostly-private setup, and it’s happening right as SpaceX keeps inching closer to a long-rumored mega IPO.

The thinking here seems bigger than just “owning an AI lab.” xAI brings models, data, and real-time social signals (via X), while SpaceX brings Starlink, launch capability, and global infrastructure. Musk has been floating ideas about vertically integrated AI — including Starlink-backed distribution and even space-based compute to dodge Earth-side power and cooling limits. Whether that’s near-term reality or classic Musk-scale ambition is… up for debate.

It also blurs lines in an interesting way: rockets, satellites, consumer internet, social media, and AI research now sitting under one roof. If this really feeds into a SpaceX IPO story, it could reshape how markets think about “AI companies” versus “infrastructure companies.” Or it could just be another chapter in the ever-expanding Musk ecosystem that regulators and competitors will be watching very closely.

-# Source: [Bloomberg — Musk’s SpaceX Combines With xAI at $1.25 Trillion Valuation](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-02-02/elon-musk-s-spacex-said-to-combine-with-xai-ahead-of-mega-ipo?utm_source=chatgpt.com)

@🥸news 

📉 Google is now selling debt that matures in the year 2126.

​Alphabet just issued a 100-year Sterling bond. This isn't a typo. They borrowed £1 Billion from UK pension funds, and they don't have to pay the principal back until your great-grandchildren are retiring.

**​The Context:**

- **​The Spend:** Google confirmed they plan to spend $185 BILLION on CapEx this year alone. They are burning through cash to build AI infrastructure at a rate that exceeds the GDP of many small nations.

- **​The Bet:** By locking in a 100-year term, Google is effectively betting that inflation will make this debt worthless by the time it matures. They get to buy GPUs today with money they'll pay back in 2126 dollars (or pounds).

**​The Historical Rhyme:**

This is extremely rare for a tech company. The last major tech firms to issue 100-year bonds were IBM (1996) and Motorola (1997).
For those who know their history: that was right before the Dot-Com crash.

​The market is officially treating Big Tech as "Critical Infrastructure"—utilities that are expected to last as long as governments. Let's see if the history books agree.

> Source: [Financial Times](https://www.ft.com/content/3260bc45-e09e-45a7-ae30-e55effbaf29b)

embeds.title: Alphabet lines up 100-year sterling bond sale
embeds.description: Deal comes as Google parent steps up AI borrowing rush with $20bn sale of dollar bonds

messages.content:
@🥸news **🚨 Breaking: OpenAI just pulled off the biggest acquihire of the year.**

If you haven't checked the news recently, Sam Altman just announced they hired Peter Steinberger—the creator of the viral open-source agent OpenClaw—to lead their new "personal agents" division.

Here is the rundown of what is actually happening behind the headlines:

- **They Didn't Buy the Repo:** OpenAI isn't taking ownership of OpenClaw to close it off. The project is moving into an independent foundation that OpenAI will sponsor. Absorbing a viral repo with 150,000+ GitHub stars and zero enterprise security would be a legal nightmare. They bought the founder and the hype, not the codebase.

- **Anthropic's Massive Fumble:** This is a masterclass in corporate self-sabotage. OpenClaw originally launched as "Clawdbot" and served as a massive, free growth engine for Anthropic's models. Instead of partnering, Anthropic's lawyers sent a Cease & Desist over the name and abruptly restricted API access. OpenAI immediately swooped in and hired the exact developer Anthropic alienated.

- **The Security Reality:** OpenClaw is an incredible proof-of-concept for local agents, but right now, it is a systems security disaster. It stores API keys in plaintext and has unauthenticated local dashboards. It is the definition of "vibe-coded" vulnerability. OpenAI wants Steinberger's vision to build a secure, polished version from scratch; they absolutely do not want his raw code running on their infrastructure.

**The Takeaway:**

Steinberger publicly stated his new mission at OpenAI is to "build an agent that even my mum can use." This is massive market validation. The AI race is officially pivoting from raw model benchmarks to building robust, agentic support tools that are actually safe and intuitive for the elderly and completely non-technical users.

The entire thesis of building autonomous tech for non-technical audiences just got validated by a $500B company. If anyone in the startup space is pitching this exact angle for the Summer accelerator batches, the timing literally could not be better.

-# Sources: [Mashable](https://mashable.com/article/openclaw-founder-peter-steinberger-joins-openai) | [InfoWorld](https://www.infoworld.com/article/4132731/openai-hires-openclaw-founder-as-ai-agent-race-intensifies-2.html)

embeds.title: OpenClaw founder joins OpenAI
embeds.description: The name change may have been a hint.

embeds.title: OpenAI hires OpenClaw founder as AI agent race intensifies
embeds.description: Peter Steinberger will lead personal agent development, while the viral open-source project will continue under an open-source foundation.

messages.content:
@🥸news **🚨 Anthropic is officially building a walled garden. The flat-rate API loophole is dead.**

For those who haven't been following the absolute chaos in the open-source agent space recently: developers have been using a clever loophole to run incredibly expensive, autonomous AI agents for cheap. Tools like OpenClaw, OpenCode, and Roo Code allowed users to log in with their $20 or $100/month Claude consumer subscriptions (via an OAuth token) instead of paying per-token on the raw API.

Anthropic just officially killed it. They updated their legal documentation to explicitly nuke the use of consumer OAuth tokens in all third-party tools.

**The History:**

This actually started in early January when users of OpenCode and Crush started getting hit with account bans for routing their Claude Max subscriptions through third-party CLIs. After weeks of community confusion, Anthropic has made the policy absolute.

**The New Reality:**

If you pay for Claude Pro ($20) or Max ($100-$200), your tokens are now locked in a cage. You are only allowed to use those tokens inside Anthropic's official web UI or their closed-source Claude Code CLI. Using popular open-source tools like OpenClaw, Cline, or NanoClaw now strictly requires raw API keys.

**The Brutal Truth (Why Anthropic Did This):**

This is the "Buffet Problem" in action. The $100 Max plan is subsidized for human-paced interactive coding. Autonomous agents, however, are compute shredders. They reload massive context windows (often 50k+ tokens) on every single recursive loop. Developers were essentially running $1,000+ worth of API inference through a $100 consumer subscription. Anthropic looked at the server costs and realized they were bleeding money to subsidize third-party bots.

If you are mapping out the unit economics for an agentic startup right now, this is the most important lesson you will get all year. You cannot build a business model on top of another company's loss-leader.

The strategy is clear: Anthropic wants to capture the entire developer ecosystem. If you want affordable inference, you have to use their proprietary frontend. Open-source orchestrators are officially being priced out of the Claude ecosystem.

-# Sources: [Hacker News Fallout](https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47069299) | [Anthropic Docs Update](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/legal-and-compliance)

embeds.title: Anthropic officially bans using subscription auth for third party use
Legal and compliance - Claude Code Docs
embeds.description: Legal agreements, compliance certifications, and security information for Claude Code.

@🥸news 🔴  **Sam Altman’s Pentagon Q&A on X exposed a policy reversal and raised serious questions about OpenAI’s safety stance.**

> “For a long time, we planned to do non-classified work only.”
> 
> This week, Altman said the Department of Defense was “flexible on what we needed.”

__That flexibility enabled OpenAI to move forward with a classified agreement.__ 
Meaning **OpenAI models will now operate inside classified U.S. government systems.**This marks a significant expansion beyond OpenAI’s earlier public positioning around limiting military entanglement.

When pressed on the contradiction, Altman argued that being “in the room” allows OpenAI to shape responsible defense use of AI rather than leaving decisions to others. Critics see this as a strategic pivot from years of safety-forward branding.

He also addressed escalating tensions in the AI ecosystem, including **threats directed at Anthropic** during the fallout.

🟠  Amid the controversy, __**Anthropic’s Claude** jumped to **No. 2 on Apple’s Top Apps** chart__, suggesting public momentum may not be aligning with OpenAI.

@🥸news  The biggest tech companies are warring over who’s responsible for children’s safety online, with billions of dollars in fines on the line as states rapidly pass conflicting laws requiring companies to verify users’ ages.

The struggle has pitted Meta Platforms Inc. and other app developers against Apple Inc. and Alphabet Inc.’s Google, the world’s largest app stores. Lobbyists for both sides are moving from state to state, working to water down or redirect the legislation to minimize their clients’ risks.

Meta supporters argue the app stores should be responsible for figuring out whether minors are accessing inappropriate content. Apple and Google, meanwhile, argue age verification laws violate children’s privacy and argue the individual apps are better-positioned to do age checks. 

Source: [some website dhruv sent](https://techoversight.org/2025/07/29/bloomberg-meta-google-lobbyists-fight-to-pass-the-buck-on-kids-online-safety/)

@🥸news @Nerds 🪟 **Windows** finally does something...__good__?

Microsoft has released a statement saying they are plannning several changes the community has requested such as: **Taskbar Freedom**, Dialing back **AI**, **less** annoying updates and also are rolling out a new app for Feedback.

There's also a *lot* of performance changes (how much **ram** background resources take), *snappier* UI, and **better network/file performance for Windows Subsystem for Linux** (WSL).

Along with that, there will be **fewer crashes**, **predictable reboots**, __better__ bluetooth/usb/printer connections, and insider builds will be tested more strictly before publishing.

**NOTE**: This is a "promise" by Microsoft, and we have yet to see any of this in execution.
